goal: Assist with communications for a new job (planning, tone, audience)
Draft two messages: private note to previous team and public announcement to professional contacts
constraints: Avoid brag-like tone; ensure a friendly sign-off and a positive look-ahead.
decisions: Tone: warm, professional; appreciative and concise.
Private note should state availability to help with handover and express gratitude.
Wording should be simple and accessible to readers outside the industry.
Tone should avoid brag-like language; maintain a friendly sign-off and a positive look-ahead.
entities: Goofy photo montage taped to monitor
Small desk plant given to newer hire
Notebook with a logo I can't pronounce
Cat cameo during last-week video call
open: Collect required details to tailor drafts (previous team/company name, new company/title, recipients for private note, publication channel for public announcement)
Note elevator conversation about calendar tips and recurring meetings to tailor drafts
Identify and gather onboarding questions the user wants clarified or reworded
